a:r.a:t: Tour quality varies significantly, making it harder for Sales Executives to convert tours into deals. Marketing is optimized for tour volume, so sales may receive low-intent or poorly qualified prospects.
Tour allocation to sales reps is uneven, creating perceived inequity in opportunity and earnings potential
Sales presentation attendance, and therefore sales outcomes, is reliant upon the ability of telemarketing and marketing departments to sell packages to customers and drive tour attendance
Assigns tours based on customer profile and rep specialization
AL reps assigned for first time buyers
IH reps assigned for current owners
Rep frames up lifetime value to potential owners
AL or IH rep briefs and hands-off the customer
TO restructures the deal to strategically position customer in a place to buy
Conducts exit interview to assess customer experience
HGV Sales, Telemarketing & Marketing Process Flows
